title: Pay the Full Statement Balance Every Month
description: Paying the Total Due in full, not just the Minimum Due, is the single biggest factor in whether a credit card saves you money or costs you money. Full payment keeps every purchase interest-free.

title: Pay Before the Due Date, Not On It
description: Paying a day or two before the due date protects you from processing delays or bank downtime causing an accidental late payment — don't cut it exactly to the deadline.

title: Keep Credit Utilization Below 30%
description: Try to keep your outstanding balance under roughly 30% of your total credit limit at any time, since high utilization can lower your credit score even if you always pay on time.

title: Track Your Spending Monthly, Not Just at Bill Time
description: Check your transactions through the app or SMS alerts as you go, rather than being surprised by the total when the statement arrives — this makes it far easier to catch overspending early.

title: Don't Spend Just to Earn Rewards
description: Reward points and cashback are only a genuine benefit if you were going to spend that money anyway. Spending extra purely to hit a rewards threshold usually costs more in interest than the reward is worth.

title: Avoid Unnecessary EMI Conversions
description: EMI conversion can help spread a large, planned expense, but converting routine purchases into EMIs out of habit adds processing fees and can hide how much debt you're actually carrying.

title: Review Every Statement Line by Line
description: Go through your statement each month to catch billing errors, unrecognized charges, or forgotten subscriptions — this is also your best defense against unnoticed fraud.

title: Enable Transaction Alerts
description: Turn on SMS or app alerts for every transaction so you know immediately if a purchase you didn't make shows up on your card.

title: Report Suspicious Transactions Immediately
description: If you see a transaction you don't recognize, contact your bank right away — most banks limit your liability for fraud only if it's reported promptly.
